concerns.text: FR-031b requires 'Ground truth MUST be established by human annotation independent of the pipeline' for real-world validation. Without access to raw data, human annotators can only verify internal consistency (same reconstruction logic as pipeline), not external accuracy. This creates circular validation: annotators reconstruct from reported numbers using same formulas, so agreement validates formula consistency, not correctness. See Methodological Limitations section which acknowledges this limitation but FR-031b still claims independent ground truth.

FR-004 uses absolute p-value difference threshold of 0.05 for inconsistency flagging. This is statistically problematic: for small p-values (e.g., 0.001), 0.05 absolute difference represents 50x significance change; for p≈0.5, it's only 10%. Standard practice uses relative error, log-odds scale, or confidence interval overlap. This threshold choice affects validity of inconsistency detection claims.

Task T044 still contains '[deferred]' placeholder: 'when any domain exceeds [deferred] of corpus' despite FR-027 explicitly requiring 30% threshold. This is a documentation regression that obscures traceability to the spec requirement. Should read '30%' to match FR-027.
